Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire process Post Job Ad Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait Screen Decide and Screen Interview but there is no edge between Interview and Decide So Interview and Decide are in parallel after Screen That means you can make a hiring decision without conducting interviews or concurrently with interviews This is a severe anomaly Also the standard order would be Screen Interview Decide Here Interview is not a predecessor of Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post Screen and Post Interview Anomaly Interview happens in parallel with or before Screen Normally you screen first then interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Decide loop onboarding Anomaly onboarding is in a loop meaning you can onboard the employee multiple times loop operator Onboard skip means do Onboard then either exit or do skip and Onboard again That means Onboard can repeat This is an anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: xor payroll Payroll is optional Anomaly adding to payroll is optional which means an employee might not get paid

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets list anomalies for Model 1 Missing edge between Interview and Decide Interview and Decide are both enabled after Screen and can be executed in parallel or Decide can be executed before Interview A hiring decision without an interview is a severe anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets list anomalies for Model 2 Screen and Interview are parallel Post Interview instead of Screen Interview This means interviewing without screening Onboarding is in a loop can be repeated Payroll is optional can be skipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Making a hiring decision without conducting an interview Model 1 is fundamentally violating the essence of the process In Model 2 interviewing without screening is bad practice but it does not completely break the core logic of the hiring decision being based on the interview

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 2 Interview is strictly before Decide So you cannot make a hiring decision without conducting an interview This is a crucial requirement

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1s anomaly is the most severe So Model 2 more closely aligns with the normative version because it correctly orders Interview before Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I will argue that Model 2 more closely aligns because it preserves the fundamental requirement that a hiring decision must follow an interview whereas Model 1 allows a decision to be made without an interview which fundamentally violates the hiring logic
